title: Entanglement driven quantum cryptography for metropolitan optical networks
tldr: Experimental findings indicate that an adaptable and resilient structure of this kind goes far beyond the conceptual frameworks of QKD modelling and real-world applications to provide insights on the necessary performance standards and to provide viable implementation guidelines to large-scale quantum cryptographic systems in the future.

title: An entropy-increasing circuit for quantum walking and its application in quantum encryption
tldr: A quantum walk-based scrambling algorithm and a shift-overlap XOR diffusion algorithm were designed, and corresponding quantum circuit implementations were provided, and the proposed method was validated through security experiments, showing that the robustness test results show that the ciphertext can effectively resist salt, pepper, and noise attacks.

title: Quantum teleportation, remote state preparation and joint remote state preparation: achieving multiple jobs through a hybrid protocol in presence of noise
tldr: This manuscript prepares a multi-tasking protocol in which the main objective is to create an interconnecting quantum network between three quantum communication protocols such as quantum teleportation, remote state preparation and joint remote state preparation by use of a single quantum network.

title: One-step state transfer between two superconducting qutrits in circuit quantum electrodynamics

On the construction of quantum circuits for S-boxes with different criteria based on the SAT solver
tldr: This work investigates the quantum circuit implementation of S-boxes based on the SAT solver with encoding methods of the logic gates and the NCT-based circuit and construct STP models to design S-boxes with given criteria configurations on implementation and properties.

title: Symmetry-constrained QCNN for few-shot learning with polylogarithmic generalization bounds
tldr: This work introduces a symmetry-constrained quantum convolutional neural network framework tailored to few-shot learning in the noisy intermediate-scale quantum (NISQ) setting, and implements a brick-layer circuit architecture with frequency collision avoidance, ensuring nearest neighbor connectivity and practical feasibility.

title: Velocity Verlet-based optimization for variational quantum eigensolvers
abstract: The variational quantum eigensolver (VQE) is a key algorithm for near-term quantum computers, yet its performance is often limited by the classical optimization of circuit parameters. We propose using the velocity Verlet algorithm, inspired by classical molecular dynamics, to address this challenge. By introducing an inertial “velocity” term, our method efficiently explores complex energy landscapes. We compare its performance against standard optimizers on H2 and LiH molecules. For H2, our method achieves chemical accuracy with fewer quantum circuit evaluations than L-BFGS-B. For LiH, it attains the lowest final energy, demonstrating its potential for high-accuracy VQE simulations.
tldr: This work proposes using the velocity Verlet algorithm, inspired by classical molecular dynamics, to address the VQE challenge by introducing an inertial “velocity” term, and compares its performance against standard optimizers on H2 and LiH molecules.

title: QGoL: Quantum Game of Life
tldr: The quantum paradigm is presented as a good candidate for modelling probabilistic behaviour, providing a convenient description of the problem and efficient calculation of the necessary probabilities in an intrinsic way, to illustrate the advantages of quantum computing for this calculation.

title: Quantum audio neural networks with time-series encoding for audio classification
tldr: This research provides a new pathway for building efficient quantum audio processing technology and demonstrates broad application potential in quantum machine learning.

title: The quantum Rock-Paper-Scissors-Lizard-Spock Show
tldr: A novel quantum implementation of Rock-Paper-Scissors and its extended variant, RPSLS, using Grover’s search algorithm is presented, demonstrating how quantum superposition and entanglement can transform classical gameplay.

title: A finite state automaton for quantum computing
tldr: This work represents the first proposal to apply quantum computing to the problem of verifying conventional systems and would facilitate software verification, program analysis, protocol design, and verification of circuits, among other applications.

title: QMin: Quantum Circuit Minimization via Gate Fusions for Efficient State Vector Simulation
tldr: A novel two-stage gate fusion strategy, namely QMin, based on the observation that control input qubits can reduce the simulation cost of a gate is proposed based on the observation that control input qubits can reduce the simulation cost of a gate.

title: Quantum collision attacks on reduced SHA-256.
tldr: This study demonstrates the conversion of a semi-free-start collision into a full collision attack through a specialized quantum technique, and establishes a new benchmark for collision attacks on hash functions.

title: Construction and optimization of quantum modular exponentiation circuits based on the V gate
tldr: This work proposes two modular exponentiation circuits based on the V gate, with further improvements introduced through the use of zero resets, which achieve reductions in circuit depth and quantum cost within their respective domains, while preserving general applicability.

title: Decoherence and quantum threats in voice biometric authentication with post-quantum countermeasures
tldr: An integrated functional system that encapsulates threat models for voice biometrics within the quantum world, integrates PQC (Post-Quantum Cryptographic) foundations with quantum resilient detection, and provides prescriptive choices for parameters upon deployment with experimentally confirmed metrics is offered.

title: Methane emissions forecasting using hybrid quantum–classical deep learning models: case study of North Africa
Efficient preparation method for arbitrary multiqubit states based on quantum walk
A quantum circuit parameterized decomposition method based on local synthesis
tldr: Experimental results demonstrate that PDBLS significantly reduces the number of 2Q basis gates across various benchmark circuits and gate sets, showing advantages under both exact and approximate decomposition conditions, and exhibiting strong scalability on large-scale circuits.

title: Ground-state energy of He atom using a four-qubit photonic processor with the variational quantum eigensolver
QC-Net: a hybrid quantum-classical neural network model for medical image segmentation
tldr: A hybrid quantum-classical neural network (QC-Net) model with a novel encoder that outperforms both U-Net and CA-Net, achieving an average Dice coefficient of 93.2%, indicating improvements of 5.43% and 1.12%, respectively.

title: A tutorial on applying quantum multiple-valued decision diagrams to circuit simulation
tldr: A comprehensive guide for implementing reversible and quantum circuit simulators using quantum multiple-Valued Decision Diagrams by integrating existing methodologies into a single cohesive framework is provided.

title: Low-depth quantum approximate optimization algorithm for maximum likelihood detection in massive MIMO
tldr: A comprehensive QAOA-based ML detection scheme for binary symbols and a Bayesian optimization-based parameters initialization algorithm to obtain initial parameters of large-scale QAOA from small-scale and classical instances, increasing the likelihood of identifying the precise solution.

title: Noise-resilient controlled quantum teleportation using quantum error correction
tldr: To enhance CQT’s noise robustness, the quantum error correction (QEC) is introduced into CQT and the protocol can be extended to use other stronger QEC code, which provides a promising way to promote CQT’s practicality in the future.

title: Enhancing the performance of variational quantum models by optimizing observable measurement based on generalization bounds
tldr: The effect of the F-norm of the measurement operator on the generalization bound of the parametric quantum machine learning model based on margin loss function is proved and a new idea for the design of quantum models is provided through the study of generalization theory.

title: Space-frequency-based multichannel dual encryption for quantum color images using chaotic system and quantum walks
tldr: This research introduces a novel approach to quantum color image encryption using a space-frequency-based multichannel dual encryption scheme, employing chaotic systems and quantum walk techniques, providing a more comprehensive solution when compared to the QIRHSI encryption scheme, which solely encrypts the intensity channel.

title: Variational quantum eigensolver toward non-bonded interaction system with hardware-efficient ansatz
tldr: The result suggests that it is possible to solve the electronic structure problem of those complicated non-bonded system with modified HEA, which improves the accuracy of ground state calculation, recovers the equilibrium geometry of molecules, and efficiently mitigates the barren plateau problem in HEA.

title: On the quantum circuit model of a kind of quantum adiabatic evolution
tldr: The implementation of the quantum algorithms given by Das et al. on the quantum circuit model is studied, and it is found that the total time slices thus needed are not necessarily equal to the time complexity of the quantum algorithm.

title: A chaotic maps-based multiparty quantum summation computation scheme for cloud environments
tldr: This protocol uses quantum homomorphic encryption based on quantum one-time pads to perform addition operations to be performed on data without decrypting it, which greatly ensures the security of the data.

title: Quantum error correction with Kolmogorov–Arnold network for rotated surface codes decoding
tldr: Empirical results consistently show that the proposed decoder outperforms MWPM and FFNN approaches, achieving superior performance on the evaluated metrics.

title: Quantum color image edge detection algorithm based on Sobel operator
tldr: The analysis shows that compared with existing related work, the algorithm in this paper has lower circuit complexity and fewer qubits, reducing the circuit complexity from O(n2+q3) to O(q).

title: Quantum alternating operator ansatz for solving the minimum dominating set problem on sparse graphs with a specific structure
tldr: A quantum algorithm based on the quantum alternating operator ansatz (QAOA+) to solve the minimum dominating set problem and can achieve a high approximation ratio with fewer circuit layers on most instances and exhibit clear performance advantages compared with the QAOA method.

title: Crosswalk traffic light detection for the visually impaired based on hybrid classical–quantum neural networks
tldr: A novel two-step method combining classical and quantum computation to accurately detect crosswalk traffic lights for visually impaired pedestrians using the improved YOLOv5 model with a newly designed parameterized quantum circuit is proposed.

title: Universal blind quantum computing assisted by quantum teleportation
tldr: This work proposes a novel BQC protocol that combines quantum teleportation with one-time-pad cryptography, effectively addressing the challenges of implementing universal BQC within the circuit model.

title: Measurement-free reconstruction circuit of quantum secrets in quantum secret sharing
abstract: For a quantum secret sharing scheme built from a general quantum stabilizer code, no measurement-free circuit has been known for reconstructing its quantum secrets, except particular classes, such as one proposed by Cleve, Gottesman and Lo. We propose a measurement-free reconstruction circuit of quantum secrets in quantum secret sharing based on stabilizer codes. Our reconstruction circuit has width k+|J|\documentclass[12pt]{minimal} \usepackage{amsmath} \usepackage{wasysym} \usepackage{amsfonts} \usepackage{amssymb} \usepackage{amsbsy} \usepackage{mathrsfs} \usepackage{upgreek} \setlength{\oddsidemargin}{-69pt} \begin{document}$$k+|J|$$\end{document} and consists of O(k|J|) one- or two-qudit unitary gates when |J| participants reconstruct k-qudit quantum secrets.
tldr: A measurement-free reconstruction circuit of quantum secrets in quantum secret sharing based on stabilizer codes, which consists of O(k|J|) one- or two-qudit unitary gates when |J| participants reconstruct k-qudit quantum secrets.
